claim: T1 is a KNOWN-mechanism / UNTESTED-failure-mode gap: spawn-on-GET-/api/health is BY DESIGN (lazy first-embed spawn), but the launcher-internal probe landing on a sibling cold demand listener under overlap was never anticipated; the probe-vs-demand discriminator was a documented OPEN QUESTION never closed.

No packet in the 006 launcher-concurrency arc or the 010 embedding-consolidation arc mentions a probe-marker header, a route allowlist, or 'probe != demand'. The probe-marker fix is net-new design with no prior art to back-port or conflict with.

T2's N-way-primary failure was explicitly ASSUMED AWAY at design time (012 risk row: lease single-writer prevents concurrent primaries, only stale-socket is real) and consolidation of the 3 copies is a DEFERRED open question; 024 integration test never covers N-way bind survival. So T2 is a designed-away + untested case, not a missing feature.

EXACT T1 fix surface pinned: emit X-Speckit-Probe:liveness into the probe request string at launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:240 (probeModelServer, launcher-internal only); check request.headers['x-speckit-probe'] in handleModelServerDemand before the unconditional launch() at model-server-supervision.cjs:1216-1218 and return a non-spawning liveness reply when present. Header survives the UDS round-trip; consumers (hf-local.ts GET /api/health :712, POST /api/embed :835) do not set it, so the lazy-spawn contract is preserved.

EXACT T2 fix surface pinned (carried + sharpened from iter-2): make canUnlinkExistingSocket race-safe (ENOENT at lstatSync :123 -> reclaimable; allowlist/uid/realpath :119-128 -> best-effort, never throw err :242-243) targeting spec-memory's permissive behavior :196-207; soften the dir-ownership statSync block :171-188; reconverge the 3 socket-server.ts copies behind one shared module (012 deferred consolidation) or back-port the identical guard as a stopgap; optionally land 018 deferred (c) lease socketPath to remove the /tmp->/private/tmp realpath divergence.

Shared root invariant: both T1 and T2 stem from a single-active-primary assumption that breaks under N-way launcher OVERLAP. T1: a launcher's internal liveness probe lands on a sibling's cold demand listener and is read as demand -> spurious spawn. T2: two owner-eligible primaries race the daemon socket bind and the loser's non-race-safe reclaim gate aborts -> orphaned inode, no listener.

Plan ordering: fix T2 first (active repeatable wedge, highest user impact: wedged secondary launchers + MCP disconnects), then T1 (give-up-marker churn / spurious crash-loop). Minimal surface = 2 files for T1 (launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs + model-server-supervision.cjs) + 2 daemon socket-server.ts copies for T2 (+ optional shared module).

Verification strategy: T2 = deterministic N>=3 owner-eligible bind race against one injected socket dir forcing EADDRINUSE + racing unlink, assert exactly one listener and no orphaned inode, never throw (add to 024 suite). T1 = unit-test handleModelServerDemand with x-speckit-probe header asserts launch() NOT called, without header asserts launch() IS called.

User-symptom mapping: T1 -> give-up-marker churn / spurious crash-loop (spurious spawn of the broken-on-this-host model server writes hf-embed-giveup.json + arms crash-loop cooldown at model-server-supervision.cjs:1197-1199) on a host that uses Ollama. T2 -> wedged ~49MB secondary code-index launchers + mk_code_index/mk_skill_advisor MCP disconnect while mk-spec-memory (permissive copy) stays connected.

The brief's path 026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/006-mcp-launcher-concurrency is STALE; the arc physically lives at 026.../003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/006-mcp-launcher-concurrency and the hf-local model-server design lives at 026.../003-memory-and-causal-runtime/010-embedding-consolidation-hf-local-server. Located both via find before reconciling.

'Suppress /api/health at the demand listener' remains RULED OUT (iter-3 F4) AND is additionally confirmed design-violating: spawn-on-first-health-GET is the INTENDED lazy trigger (004/implementation-summary.md:50, REQ-001 :126); suppressing it would break the documented lazy-spawn contract for genuine hf-local hosts, not just the wake path.

onnxruntime-common topology break is confirmed ORTHOGONAL and out of scope: portability-only, matters only on non-Ollama hosts; this host uses Ollama (strategy NON-GOAL section 4). Neither T1 nor T2 fix touches it.
